metadata.description: The display name of the workbook.

content.json:
# AVS Syslog Monitoring Dashboard

This workbook provides visibility into VMware syslog events flowing into the **AVSSyslog** table from your Azure VMware Solution (AVS) private cloud.

- **Part 1** — Severity-based monitoring (Emergency, Alert, Critical, Error)
- **Part 2** — Event-specific monitoring (host failures, VM changes, DNS, firewall, etc.)
- **Host Health** — Per-host Severity breakdown
- **Pipeline Health** — Ingestion heartbeat and volume

---

parameters.name: Workspace
parameters.query:
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces"
| project id, name, location, resourceGroup
| order by name asc
parameters.label: Log Analytics Workspace

parameters.name: HostFilter
parameters.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| distinct HostName
| order by HostName asc
parameters.label: Host

content.json:
## Deploy AVS Syslog Alerts

[![Deploy to Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://portal.azure.com/#create/Microsoft.Template/uri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fbrenmelo%2Favs-syslog-monitoring%2Fmain%2Favs-syslog-alerts-deploy-template.json/createUIDefinitionUri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Fbrenmelo%2Favs-syslog-monitoring%2Fmain%2FcreateUiDefinition.json)

Click the button above to launch a guided wizard that lets you pick your workspace, action groups, and which alerts to deploy.

---

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| summarize c = count()
| extend msgs = iff(c > 0,
    dynamic(['✅ AVSSyslog table detected — dashboard is ready.']),
    dynamic(['⚠️ AVSSyslog table not found. Ensure your AVS private cloud sends syslogs to this workspace via a Diagnostic Setting.', '➡️ Steps: AVS private cloud → Diagnostic settings → Add diagnostic setting → select Syslog category → send to this workspace.']))
| mv-expand Status = msgs to typeof(string)
| project Status

content.json:
---
## Overview — Severity Distribution

Syslog uses eight standard severity levels (RFC 5424). This dashboard highlights **Severity 0–3** as high-impact events:

| Level | Keyword | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| **0** | `emerg` / `emergency` | System is unusable |
| **1** | `alert` | Immediate action required |
| **2** | `crit` / `critical` | Critical condition (e.g. hardware failure) |
| **3** | `err` / `error` | Error condition |
| 4 | `warning` | Warning — may indicate a developing issue |
| 5 | `notice` | Normal but noteworthy |
| 6 | `info` | Informational |
| 7 | `debug` | Debug-level detail |

content.query:
let tableExists = toscalar(union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime)[]) | summarize count()) > 0;
let actual = union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity in ("emerg", "emergency", "alert", "crit", "critical", "err", "error")
| extend SeverityGroup = case(
    Severity in ("emerg", "emergency"), "Emergency (0)",
    Severity == "alert", "Alert (1)",
    Severity in ("crit", "critical"), "Critical (2)",
    Severity in ("err", "error"), "Error (3)",
    "")
| summarize Count = count() by SeverityGroup;
let defaults = datatable(SeverityGroup:string, Count:long) [
    "Emergency (0)", 0,
    "Alert (1)", 0,
    "Critical (2)", 0,
    "Error (3)", 0
];
union actual, defaults
| summarize Count = sum(Count) by SeverityGroup
| extend Status = case(not(tableExists), "⚠️ No Data", Count == 0, "🟢 HEALTHY", "")
| extend SortOrder = case(SeverityGroup == "Emergency (0)", 0, SeverityGroup == "Alert (1)", 1, SeverityGroup == "Critical (2)", 2, SeverityGroup == "Error (3)", 3, 99)
| order by SortOrder asc
| project SeverityGroup, Count, Status
content.title: High-Impact Events by Severity (0–3 only)

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| summarize Count = count() by Severity
| order by case(
    Severity == "emerg", 0,
    Severity == "emergency", 0,
    Severity == "alert", 1,
    Severity == "crit", 2,
    Severity == "critical", 2,
    Severity == "err", 3,
    Severity == "error", 3,
    Severity == "warning", 4,
    Severity == "notice", 5,
    Severity == "info", 6,
    Severity == "debug", 7,
    99
  ) asc
content.title: Full Severity Distribution (all levels)

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| summarize Count = count() by AppName
| top 15 by Count desc
content.title: Top Event Sources (AppName — the VMware process or service that generated the syslog entry)

content.json:
---
## Part 1 — Severity-Based Monitoring (emergency / alert / critical / error)

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity in ("emerg", "emergency", "alert", "crit", "critical", "err", "error")
| summarize Count = count() by Severity, bin(TimeGenerated, {TimeRange:grain})
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: Severity 0–3 Events Over Time

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity in ("emerg", "emergency")
| project TimeGenerated, HostName, AppName, Facility, Severity, Message
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100
content.title: 🔴 Emergency Events (Severity 0 — emerg): System is unusable

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity == "alert"
| project TimeGenerated, HostName, AppName, Facility, Severity, Message
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100
content.title: 🔴 Alert Events (Severity 1 — alert): Immediate action required

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity in ("crit", "critical")
| project TimeGenerated, HostName, AppName, Facility, Severity, Message
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100
content.title: 🟠 Critical Events (Severity 2 — crit): Critical condition

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity in ("err", "error")
| summarize ErrorCount = count() by HostName, AppName, bin(TimeGenerated, {TimeRange:grain})
| order by TimeGenerated desc
content.title: 🟡 Error Events Over Time by Host + App (Severity 3 — err): Error condition

content.json:
#### Understanding the error count

A high number of **error**-level events is common in VMware/ESXi environments and usually does **not** indicate a critical problem. VMware services such as `vpxa`, `hostd`, `vobd`, and `rhttpproxy` frequently log transient, self-resolving conditions (SOAP timeouts, temporary connection retries, NTP drift, VSAN rebalancing) at error severity. A single noisy service retrying every 30 seconds can generate thousands of identical entries per day.

Use the **Errors by Source** and **Top Repeated Error Messages** panels below to identify whether the count is driven by a few repeating patterns (normal noise) or by many distinct messages (worth investigating). The repeated-message panel removes the leading timestamp and host prefix, then groups on the first 120 characters of the remaining message text so recurring errors are counted together more accurately.

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity in ("err", "error")
| summarize Count = count() by AppName
| order by Count desc
| take 15
content.title: Errors by Source (AppName)

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity in ("err", "error")
| extend NormalizedMessage = replace_regex(Message, @"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}T[^\s]+\s+\S+\s+", "")
| extend MessagePreview = substring(NormalizedMessage, 0, 120)
| summarize Count = count(), Hosts = dcount(HostName), FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated) by AppName, MessagePreview
| order by Count desc
| take 20
content.title: Top Repeated Error Messages (grouped by message text; first 120 characters shown)

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity in ("err", "error")
| project TimeGenerated, HostName, AppName, Facility, Severity, Message
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 200
content.title: 🟡 Error Events Detail (Severity 3 — err): Error condition

content.json:
---
## Part 2 — Event-Specific Monitoring

These panels track specific VMware events by searching the **Message** field for known vCenter event names (e.g. `VmRemovedEvent`, `hostshutdownevent`). Counts reflect how many syslog entries matched each pattern — some events (like VM inventory changes) can be routine and high-volume.

#### What these event panels mean

- **Host Events (connection lost, shutdown, added, connected)**: Tracks major host lifecycle and connectivity changes. A lost connection or shutdown may indicate host instability, maintenance, or an outage; added/connected events usually indicate recovery, expansion, or re-registration.
- **DNS Failures Over Time**: Shows failed DNS lookups from VMware components. Persistent failures can break name resolution for services, updates, cluster operations, or external integrations.
- **Distributed Firewall Blocked Traffic Over Time**: Shows traffic denied by the NSX distributed firewall. Some blocks are expected by policy, but spikes or unexpected blocks can indicate misconfiguration or application connectivity problems.
- **Host Maintenance Mode Changes**: Tracks hosts entering or exiting maintenance mode. This is often expected during patching or operational work, but unexpected changes can explain VM movement, reduced capacity, or service impact.
- **Role / Permission Changes (Security Audit)**: Tracks permission or role assignment changes such as `RoleAddedEvent`. These are important for security review because they may reflect legitimate admin activity or unauthorized privilege changes.

content.title: Event-Specific Summary

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Message has_any ("lost connection to the host", "hostshutdownevent", "hostaddedevent", "hostconnectedevent")
| project TimeGenerated, HostName, AppName, Facility, Severity, Message
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100
content.title: Host Events (connection lost, shutdown, added, connected)

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Message has_any ("vmdisconnectedevent", "vmremovedevent", "VmGuestRebootEvent", "VmPowerStateChangedEvent", "vmcreatedevent")
| project TimeGenerated, HostName, AppName, Facility, Severity, Message
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100
content.title: VM Events (disconnected, deleted, rebooted, power change, created)

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where AppName == "dnsmasq" and Message has "Failed DNS Query"
| summarize FailureCount = count() by HostName, bin(TimeGenerated, {TimeRange:grain})
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: DNS Failures Over Time

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where AppName == "FIREWALL" or ProcId == "FIREWALL"
| where Message has_any ("DROP", "REJECT", "denied")
| summarize BlockedCount = count() by HostName, bin(TimeGenerated, {TimeRange:grain})
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: Distributed Firewall Blocked Traffic Over Time

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Message has_any ("The host has entered maintenance mode", "The host has exited maintenance mode")
| project TimeGenerated, HostName, AppName, Facility, Severity, Message
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 50
content.title: Host Maintenance Mode Changes

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Message has "RoleAddedEvent"
| project TimeGenerated, HostName, AppName, Facility, Severity, Message
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 50
content.title: Role / Permission Changes (Security Audit)

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity in ("emerg", "emergency", "alert", "crit", "critical", "err", "error")
| summarize
    emerg = countif(Severity in ("emerg", "emergency")),
    alert = countif(Severity == "alert"),
    crit = countif(Severity in ("crit", "critical")),
    err = countif(Severity in ("err", "error")),
    Total = count()
  by HostName
| order by Total desc
content.title: High-Impact Events per Host (Severity 0–3)

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where '*' in ({HostFilter}) or HostName in~ ({HostFilter})
| where Severity in ("emerg", "emergency", "alert", "crit", "critical", "err", "error")
| summarize Count = count() by HostName, bin(TimeGenerated, {TimeRange:grain})
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: High-Impact Events per Host Over Time

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| summarize LastEvent = max(TimeGenerated)
| extend MinutesSinceLastEvent = iff(isnull(LastEvent), -1, datetime_diff('minute', now(), LastEvent))
| extend Status = case(
    isnull(LastEvent), '🔴 NO DATA — AVSSyslog table is empty or not found',
    MinutesSinceLastEvent > 30, '🔴 NO DATA — Pipeline may be down',
    MinutesSinceLastEvent > 15, '🟡 DELAYED — Check pipeline',
    '🟢 HEALTHY')
| project Status, LastEvent, MinutesSinceLastEvent
content.title: AVSSyslog Ingestion Heartbeat

content.query:
union isfuzzy=true AVSSyslog, (datatable(TimeGenerated:datetime, HostName:string, AppName:string, Facility:string, Severity:string, Message:string, ProcId:string)[])
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize EventCount = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: AVSSyslog Ingestion Volume (events per 5 minutes)
